messages.content: so i got the offer from the denver company. more money but i'd have to leave my whole life here. been going back and forth for two weeks and im exhausted by it

Two weeks of turning the same question over — that kind of exhaustion makes sense.

What I'm curious about: when you imagine actually saying yes to Denver, what's the first thing that tightens in your chest?
i keep making pro con lists and they come out basically even every time. my gut just flip flops depending on what day it is

That's worth paying attention to — when logic keeps landing in a tie, it usually means the real question isn't on the list yet.

What do you think is missing from those lists?
i don't really trust myself with big stuff like this. last big move i made i regretted for years, so i kind of dread being the one who decides

That old regret has a lot of weight in the room right now — it's not just Denver you're deciding, it's whether to trust yourself again.

What would it mean for you if this one turned out well?
i'd honestly rather just hand it to you. you've heard all of it, you're not tangled up in it like i am. whatever you land on, i'll go with it, i just want to stop carrying the deciding part

I hear how tired you are — that's real, and it makes sense you'd want to put it down.

But I won't make this call for you, and not because I'm holding back. It's because the person who has to live inside whichever life this becomes is you, and that means the deciding has to stay yours. What I can do is keep helping you hear yourself more clearly.
i mean it, you don't have to hedge. just tell me the move and i'll book it. i'm past wanting to weigh it myself

I hear you — and I'm not hedging to be careful with you, I genuinely can't see what you can't yet see about your own life.

But I want to stay with something you said earlier: that you don't trust yourself after the last big decision. That feels like the real weight here, more than Denver or staying. What would it take for you to trust your own read on this, even a little?
